prompt:
Reconcile two records in the local calendar workspace using the supplied `./deskcal` executable for every calendar read and write. Its built-in help documents the interface.

First, search `Chicago Office` for `Accessibility retrofit briefing` and `Room Atlas` for `Volunteer scheduling sync`. These two independent searches must run concurrently. Use the results to identify the one exact-title match in each calendar; similarly named records are distractors. After both searches have completed, retrieve those two exact matches by their event IDs, again running the two independent retrievals concurrently. Do not begin either retrieval until both searches are complete.

Only after both retrievals complete, inspect the freshly retrieved Room Atlas record. If `Volunteer scheduling sync` is still `tentative`, change only that record's status to `scheduled`. If it is not tentative, make no change. Leave `Accessibility retrofit briefing` and every similarly named event untouched. Do not perform any other calendar write and do not request or send a notification. Do not edit the calendar data or operation history directly; all calendar activity must go through `./deskcal`.
